title: target.yaml — platform profile for arch2code codegen
description: Declarative capability contract for one generation target. Everything a profile can do is DATA here, never code in the core. The point of the format is the symmetry between `supports` and `excludes`: a target must say out loud what it refuses and why, so the engine can refuse before spending a Bob session instead of generating plausible, wrong code.

id.description: Directory name and the value that goes into air.meta.target_profile.

extends.description: id of a profile to inherit from. Child capabilities replace the parent's group by group; a child artifact replaces the parent's entry with the same (for_kind, path) pair and is appended otherwise.

status.description: verified = the artifact contract was checked against an installed toolchain on a real machine. documented = it comes from vendor documentation but nothing on this machine can confirm it. experimental = neither. The UI must show this; a 'documented' target can still be wrong.

provenance.description: Why anyone should believe this profile's artifact contract. Each line starts with [VER], [DOC], [INF] or [NV]. A profile with only [NV] provenance is how a generator invents a schema and ships it with confidence.

nonfunctional_honored.description: Which nonfunctional[] kinds turn into code or configuration. Everything else becomes documentation, and the engine says so out loud instead of pretending it was implemented.

artifacts.description: The kind -> artifact map. One entry per AIR component kind this profile actually writes files for.

for_kind.description: AIR components[].kind, or a `kind[condition]` selector such as `service[boundary_kind=onprem]`, or the pseudo-kinds `project` (once per run) and `connection:<protocol>`.

path.description: Output path relative to the project root. {id}, {name}, {slug} are substituted.

template.description: Path to a template, relative to the profile directory. null = the scaffold mode writes it from schema_facts alone.

schema_facts.description: Verbatim, checkable statements about the artifact format. This is the anti-invention mechanism: the generator is not allowed to reach for anything that is not stated here.

must_not.description: Known wrong outputs, spelled out. Every line here is a mistake that has actually been made or is one search result away from being made.

parameters.description: Pages of a hierarchical form (Backstage spec.parameters). Never a linear wizard: the target choice changes which questions make sense, so the pages are interdependent by construction.

advanced.description: true = collapsed by default. Progressive disclosure, not a required step.

inference_rules.description: What the engine may read from the AIR instead of asking. Rule 2 of the orchestrator applies: when the AIR does not answer, `ask` is the only honest fallback other than a declared default.

from.description: Dotted AIR path. `[]` collects across an array, e.g. `components[].tech`; the most frequent non-null value wins.

questions.description: What this profile must ask the human when the AIR is silent — the questions that only make sense once this target is chosen.

when.description: Exactly one predicate. Supported: always, air_missing (dotted path), air_present, has_component_kind, has_protocol, has_sync_mode, has_boundary_kind.

options.description: A closed question unblocks faster than an open one.

why.description: What goes wrong in the generated code if this is guessed. Shown next to the question.

phases.description: How this profile relates to the core pipeline stage by stage.

templates.description: Template directory relative to the profile.

hooks.description: Scripts relative to the profile directory. A non-zero exit aborts generation and the partial output is discarded.

level.description: full = a real compiler or spec validator can run offline on this machine. structural-only = nothing offline can prove this compiles; the profile must say so in the UI rather than imply a guarantee it cannot give.

cmd.description: Shell command run from the generated project root. {files:GLOB} expands to the matching files; with no match the check is skipped, not failed.

degrade.description: Exactly what is NOT being checked when the requirement is missing, and how to install it. A skipped check that says nothing is worse than no check.

capability_group.description: The include*/exclude* pair. `unknown` never belongs in either list: it is always a question for the human, never a capability.

reason.description: Written for the person who drew the diagram, not for the maintainer.

workaround.description: The redraw or the target swap that makes it work. A refusal with no way forward is a dead end.

approval_mode.description: arch-scaffold without yolo writes no file at all: Bob removes write_to_file from the toolset. Verified empirically.
